title: avena sterilis subsp. byzantina
intro: Avena sterilis subsp. byzantina, also known as wild oat or oat grass, is a subspecies of oat grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Avena sterilis subsp. byzantina include relieving stress, anxiety, and insomnia, as well as soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in herbal remedies for its calming and sleep-promoting properties.
